rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative dibrominative cyclization of 1,n-enynes using bromide ionic liquid as Br source and 4-iodotoluene as redox mediator to give vinyl/alkyl dibrominated heterocycles.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (Br-) to bromine radical (Br•), which combines with 4-iodotoluene to form a hypervalent ArIBr2 (aryl iodine I/III) mediator; cathode reduces HFIP/H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: vinyl bromide / alkyl bromide cyclized product 2a

electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized to Br•, which reacts with 4-iodotoluene to generate the active ArIBr2 hypervalent iodine(III) species; ArIBr2 engages the enyne to deliver two Br atoms via cyclization and C-Br bond formation, regenerating ArI. At the cathode, protons from HFIP are reduced to H2.

probe_reagents.name: radical trap control experiments
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited, supporting a free-radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Br- and 4-iodotoluene
probe_reagents.observation: Br- oxidizes prior to Br• formation; reduction peak for Br- disappears upon addition of 4-iodotoluene, indicating reaction between Br• and 4-iodotoluene to form ArIBr2.

notes: Recyclability demonstrated: [C2MPr]Br ionic liquid recovered (91%) via liquid-liquid extraction with bromoethane and reused for 5 cycles. Downstream derivatization (Suzuki with ArB(OH)2, SN2 with NaN3 and KI) selectively replaces the alkyl bromide while leaving the vinyl bromide intact, consistent with two electronically distinct C-Br bonds in the product.
